title: PathItem
description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema10.title: Operation
__schema10.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema11.title: tags
__schema11.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema13.title: summary
__schema13.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema14.title: description
__schema14.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema15.title: operationId
__schema15.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema16.title: parameters
__schema16.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema18.title: Reference Object
__schema18.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema19.title: summary
__schema19.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema20.title: description
__schema20.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema23.title: name
__schema23.description: The name of the parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema24.title: in
__schema24.description: Location of the parameter. 'path' parameters must have required=true.

__schema25.title: description
__schema25.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema30.description: For query parameters only. Allows empty values (?param=).
__schema30.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema32.title: style
__schema32.description: Serialization style. Defaults vary by 'in': query/cookie='form', path/header='simple'.

__schema33.title: explode
__schema33.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema34.description: For query parameters. When true, allows reserved characters without encoding.
__schema34.title: allowReserved

__schema37.title: Schema Object
__schema37.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema38.title: Type Array Schema
__schema38.description: OpenAPI 3.1 (JSON Schema) allows type to be an array of primitive types.

__schema39.title: title
__schema39.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema40.title: description
__schema40.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema41.title: default
__schema41.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema42.title: example
__schema42.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema45.title: enum
__schema45.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema47.title: const
__schema47.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema49.title: Discriminator
__schema49.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema50.title: propertyName
__schema50.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema51.title: mapping
__schema51.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema56.title: XML
__schema56.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema57.title: name
__schema57.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema58.title: namespace
__schema58.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema59.title: prefix
__schema59.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema60.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema60.title: attribute

__schema62.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema62.title: wrapped

__schema66.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema66.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema67.title: description
__schema67.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema68.title: url
__schema68.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema70.title: readOnly
__schema70.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema71.title: writeOnly
__schema71.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema72.title: deprecated
__schema72.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema76.title: String Object
__schema76.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema78.title: format
__schema78.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema79.title: Pattern
__schema79.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema84.title: Number Object
__schema84.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema86.title: format
__schema86.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema87.title: multipleOf
__schema87.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema89.title: minimum
__schema89.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema91.title: maximum
__schema91.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema93.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema93.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema95.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema95.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema97.title: Integer Object
__schema97.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema99.title: format
__schema99.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema100.title: multipleOf
__schema100.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema102.title: minimum
__schema102.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema104.title: maximum
__schema104.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema106.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema106.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema108.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema108.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema110.title: Boolean Object
__schema110.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema112.title: Null Object
__schema112.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema114.title: Array Object
__schema114.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema118.title: prefixItems
__schema118.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema120.title: contains
__schema120.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema132.title: Object Object
__schema132.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema156.title: Composition Schema
__schema156.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema157.title: allOf
__schema157.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema159.title: anyOf
__schema159.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema161.title: oneOf
__schema161.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema163.title: not
__schema163.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema165.title: if
__schema165.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema169.title: else
__schema169.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema172.title: examples
__schema172.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema179.title: externalValue
__schema179.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema181.title: content
__schema181.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema183.title: MediaType
__schema183.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema191.title: Encoding
__schema191.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema192.title: contentType
__schema192.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema195.title: Header
__schema195.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema196.title: Header
__schema196.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema197.title: description
__schema197.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema198.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema198.title: required

__schema200.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema200.title: deprecated

__schema202.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema202.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema204.title: style
__schema204.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema242.title: Response
__schema242.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema243.title: Response
__schema243.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema251.title: Link
__schema251.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema252.title: Link
__schema252.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema253.title: operationId
__schema253.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema254.title: operationRef
__schema254.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema255.title: parameters
__schema255.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema258.title: requestBody
__schema258.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema262.title: url
__schema262.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema263.title: description
__schema263.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema264.title: variables
__schema264.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema266.title: ServerVariable
__schema266.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema267.title: enum
__schema267.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema269.title: default
__schema269.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema270.title: description
__schema270.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema279.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema280.title: security
__schema280.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema281.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema281.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.
